text: The meaning of this difficult passage seems to be that there is no smoke without fire; the lesser judgements already experienced by Samaria (4.6-11) are sure proof that it has incurred God’s anger, and worse calamity is to follow.

The insinuation is not clear, perhaps a general warning against luxury is intended (cf. verse 15). But this seems to be the natural rendering of a sentence that has produced a wide variety of interpretations. It runs, literally: ‘So shall the sons of Israel be dragged out, that dwell in Samaria on the corner of a bed, and in Damascus … a couch’. There is no evidence that the stuff called ‘damask’ was known, or was so called, in Amos’ time.
